title: DFIRCLI Artifact frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of an artifact file under data/artifacts/<substrate>/<Name>.md. The body (Markdown below the closing '---' fence) is prose and not validated.

name.description: Artifact identifier. Must match the filename without the .md extension and must be unique across the entire artifacts tree.

title-description.description: One-line human-readable title shown in the details-panel header for this artifact. For windows-evtx artifacts the viewer prepends 'winevtx - '.

aliases.description: Alternative names analysts may use for this artifact. Integers are allowed (e.g., 4624) for event-id shorthand. Included in search/filter but not rendered as node labels.

link.description: Primary link-affinity classification — drives the node's primary color in link-driver mode.

link-secondary.description: Optional secondary link-affinity — drives the half-node color in link-driver mode.

tags.description: Free-form tags for filtering. Common conventions: itm:<section>, persistence-primary, tamper-signal, per-user, rotation-fast, legacy-system.

substrate.description: The substrate this artifact lives in. Must match a file under data/substrates/.

substrate-instance.description: The specific substrate instance name (e.g., 'SOFTWARE', 'NTUSER.DAT', 'Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational'). Free-form — naming is validated informally against the substrate definition's known-instances list if present.

volatility.description: Natural lifetime of the evidence — how long it persists independent of adversarial behavior. Permanent = requires destructive action to remove. Persistent = survives reboot but admin/user-mutable. Session-scoped = lost on logoff/reboot. Runtime = lost on process exit.

interaction-required.description: Whether the artifact's creation requires user involvement. None = kernel/service writes without any user context. User-session = requires a logged-in user session (but no specific user action). User-action = requires explicit user action (Explorer interaction, file open, etc.).

platform.description: OS version range(s) where this artifact is present. Common keys are 'windows' and 'windows-server'; 'linux', 'macos', 'cloud', and 'note' are also accepted.

location.description: Where to find this artifact on disk / in memory. Open-shape — keys reflect whatever naming the container uses (path / hive / channel / event-id / addressing / note / live-access / etc).

fields.description: The forensically-meaningful fields within this artifact. Each field describes where it lives, what it encodes, and optionally which concept(s) it references.

observations.description: Tier-1 single-artifact claims. Each entry names a forensic proposition, the Casey certainty ceiling this artifact alone achieves for it, and an optional confidence marker describing the epistemic status of the source knowledge.

anti-forensic.description: Tamper posture of the artifact: how integrity is enforced, who can modify it, known cleaners, and signals that reveal tampering.

integrity-mechanism.description: How the substrate protects the record from modification (append-only, kernel-mediated, chunked checksums, none).

write-privilege.description: Privilege level required to modify the artifact.

provenance.description: Source attribution. Each item is either a source-registry ID (string) or an object {source, section?, note?} for citing a specific part of a source. Every ID must resolve to an entry in schema/sources.yaml.

exit-node.description: Exit-node claim: this artifact terminates a tier-2 convergence. Accepts either a bare boolean (legacy form — `false` implicit) or a structured object carrying the attribution required to defend the claim. Structured form is preferred for `is-terminus: true` — flipping the flag without sources/reasoning/implications is not supported because exit-node is an authored knowledge claim, not a derived property. Authorship typically cites tier-2/3 sources (methodology, peer-reviewed analysis, case studies).

is-terminus.description: True iff this artifact is a reasoning-terminus for at least one convergence.

terminates.description: Convergence names this artifact closes (must match entries in convergences/).

sources.description: Source IDs backing the exit-node claim. Typically tier-2/3 (methodology, peer-reviewed, case studies). Every ID must resolve in schema/sources.yaml. REQUIRED (non-empty) when is-terminus: true — a knowledge claim without attribution is incoherent. This is the ONLY per-field source requirement in the corpus; all other attribution is entity-level via provenance.

reasoning.description: Why this artifact qualifies as a terminus — the chain-closure mechanism (what inputs it provides, why downstream correlation is unnecessary). REQUIRED when is-terminus: true.

implications.description: Real-world forensic consequence — what an analyst can prove/report using this artifact as the chain terminus. The "so what" of the claim. REQUIRED when is-terminus: true.

preconditions.description: Conditions under which the terminus claim holds (e.g. "UserSid != S-1-5-18" excludes system-account events).

identifier-terminals-referenced.description: Which identifier-terminal concepts this artifact ties together (UserSID, DeviceSerial, VolumeGUID, EmailAddress, IPAddress, URL, etc.).

primary-source.description: Single source ID (from schema/sources.yaml) identified during the exit-node audit as the strongest single-source attribution for this terminus claim. Must appear in `sources`.

attribution-sentence.description: Verbatim sentence extracted from primary-source during the exit-node audit that justifies the terminus claim in real-world forensic terms.

min.description: Earliest supporting OS version (e.g., '7', 'Vista', '10', 'NT5.1'). String is acceptable because OS labels are not numeric-sortable in general.

max.description: Latest supporting OS version (e.g., '11', '2022', 'N/A — Server-only feature').

platformRangeOrLabel.description: Either a {min,max,note} object or a free-form label (e.g., 'N/A (client-only)', 'Server 2012+').

name.description: Field identifier — unique within this artifact.

kind.description: Field semantic kind. Affects downstream analytics. The enum reflects the observed vocabulary across the artifact corpus — see schema/concepts.yaml for concept kinds (identifier / value-type) which are distinct.

location.description: Free-form description of where in the container this field lives (e.g., 'hive-path\value', 'EventData → FieldName', 'offset 0x24').

type.description: Storage type if applicable (REG_DWORD, REG_SZ, uint32, FILETIME, etc.).

references-data.description: Concept(s) this field references. Each entry is validated against schema/concepts.yaml — unknown concepts or roles produce build warnings.

concept.description: Must match a concept in schema/concepts.yaml.

role.description: Must match one of that concept's declared roles.

proposition.description: UPPER_SNAKE proposition label (e.g., CONFIGURED, EXECUTED, HAD_FILE, COMMUNICATED, HAD_CREDENTIAL).

ceiling.description: Casey C-scale certainty ceiling: C1 (weakest) through C5 (strongest). A range like 'C3-C4' is allowed to indicate 'with corroboration'.

confidence.description: Epistemic status of the source knowledge supporting this observation. Orthogonal to Casey ceiling. Default 'established' when omitted.

debate-note.description: When confidence is 'debated', a short narrative capturing where the disagreement sits (which sources disagree, what the contested interpretation is).

provenanceRef.description: Either a bare source-registry ID (string) or an object pointing at a specific section of a source.

source.description: Source-registry ID matching an entry in schema/sources.yaml.

section.description: Specific section, page, or topic within the source that informs this reference.

note.description: Optional authorial note — e.g., 'primary authority' or 'disagrees with Smith (2021)'.
